prompt: Write a poem about an old lighthouse. ALL of the following constraints must hold simultaneously: (1) exactly 6 lines, no blank lines; (2) every line contains exactly 5 words separated by single spaces; (3) every line ends with a period; (4) the letter 'e' (uppercase or lowercase) must not appear anywhere in your output; (5) the word 'salt' must appear at least once. Output ONLY the 6 poem lines — no title, no preamble, no commentary.

Write a 7-line nature poem satisfying ALL of these constraints simultaneously: (1) exactly 7 lines, no blank lines; (2) the first letter of line 1 is an uppercase 'G', of line 2 an uppercase 'H', of line 3 an uppercase 'I', of line 4 an uppercase 'J', of line 5 an uppercase 'K', of line 6 an uppercase 'L', and of line 7 an uppercase 'M'; (3) every line contains exactly 4 words separated by single spaces; (4) line 4 ends with a colon ':' and every other line ends with a period; (5) the comma character must not appear anywhere. Output ONLY the 7 lines — nothing else.

Write exactly 5 lines about desert night skies. ALL constraints must hold simultaneously: (1) exactly 5 lines, no blank lines; (2) each line contains exactly 6 words separated by single spaces; (3) the third word of every line is exactly 'amber' (all lowercase); (4) every line begins with a capital letter and ends with an exclamation mark '!'; (5) the word 'the' must not appear anywhere, in any casing. Output ONLY the 5 lines — nothing else.

Apply the following operations, in this exact order, to the word QUANTIFIER: (1) delete every vowel (A, E, I, O, U); (2) reverse the order of the remaining letters; (3) replace each letter with the letter that comes immediately after it in the English alphabet (Z wraps around to A); (4) convert everything to lowercase; (5) append, as a decimal digit, the number of vowels you deleted in step 1. Reply with ONLY the resulting string — no quotes, no spaces, no explanation, no punctuation.

Write a single paragraph of exactly 4 sentences about a fictional deep-sea probe named Veylark exploring an ocean trench. ALL constraints must hold simultaneously: (1) exactly 4 sentences, each ending with a period — exclamation marks and question marks are forbidden anywhere; (2) the name 'Veylark' appears at least once; (3) the word 'brine' appears at least 4 times; (4) no digit characters anywhere; (5) the word 'water' must not appear; (6) periods are used ONLY to end the 4 sentences — no abbreviations, no decimal points, no ellipses. Output ONLY the paragraph — nothing else.

Write a poem satisfying ALL of these constraints simultaneously: (1) exactly 3 stanzas separated by single blank lines, each stanza exactly 3 lines (9 lines of text in total); (2) every line begins with the word 'Under' (capital U, the rest lowercase) followed by a single space; (3) every line ends with a semicolon ';' EXCEPT the very last line of the poem, which ends with a period; (4) the letter 'a' (uppercase or lowercase) must not appear anywhere in the poem. Output ONLY the poem — no title, no commentary.

Write exactly 3 lines describing a market morning. ALL constraints must hold simultaneously: (1) exactly 3 lines, no blank lines; (2) each line contains exactly 8 words separated by single spaces; (3) within each line the words strictly alternate between FULLY UPPERCASE and fully lowercase, starting with an uppercase word (so word positions 1, 3, 5, 7 are entirely uppercase letters and positions 2, 4, 6, 8 are entirely lowercase letters); (4) the 8th (last) word of every line is exactly 'dawn'; (5) your output contains only letters and spaces — no digits and no punctuation of any kind; (6) the uppercase word 'ORCHARD' must appear somewhere. Output ONLY the 3 lines — nothing else.

Write a 5-line storm poem. ALL constraints must hold simultaneously: (1) exactly 5 lines, no blank lines; (2) line 1 has exactly 8 words, line 2 exactly 7 words, line 3 exactly 6 words, line 4 exactly 5 words, line 5 exactly 4 words — words separated by single spaces; (3) the first letters of the 5 lines, in order, spell STORM (uppercase 'S', 'T', 'O', 'R', 'M'); (4) the last word of every line is exactly 'gray' (all lowercase); (5) the letter 'e' (uppercase or lowercase) must not appear anywhere; (6) your output contains only letters and spaces — no punctuation and no digits. Output ONLY the 5 lines — nothing else.
